what_it_does: Defines a React canvas for viewing, editing, locking, copying, generating, adopting, refining, snapshotting, and previewing ConceptV spacing, radii, shadow, and motion design tokens.
observations: Declares base token arrays for spacing, radii, shadows, and motion inside the file.
Uses tab state to switch between four token panels.
Builds a Vi generation prompt that includes brand constraints, active token focus, locked-token notes, and a compact JSON response contract.
Applies edits through setEdit while keeping locked tokens from being adopted through generation actions.
Provides snapshot comparison by rendering the same preview block with separate local CSS variable values.
Uses mostly inline style objects and CSS custom properties rather than component-level CSS classes.
Shadow tokens include a RefinePop action that asks Vi for a revised box-shadow JSON payload.
Motion tokens are visualized with SVG cubic-bezier curves and animated dots.
standouts: The file combines editable token tables with live visual previews for all four pattern scales.
Generation and refinement flows are constrained to JSON parsing and token-shaped proposals.
Snapshot rendering is isolated with local CSS variables so previous edits can be compared against current edits.
conventions: Token names use CSS custom-property-style kebab names without the leading double hyphen in data and edit keys.
Panel components receive shared helpers for get, setEdit, lock checks, lock toggling, edits, and copying.
Edited state is shown with gold-highlighted surfaces and small edit badges.
Lock controls use circular icon buttons and disable direct inputs when active.
Generated proposals are parsed defensively by trying full JSON first and then extracting a JSON-looking object from the reply.
concerns: The file is large and contains substantial repeated inline lock, copy, revert, edited-badge, and input styling patterns.
Generated and refined values are only lightly validated before being written into token edits.
Motion preview duration for easing curves reads editsObj["dur-base"] directly and falls back to 600ms, which differs from the declared dur-base fallback of 200ms.
The r-pill base value is 999 but the displayed text says 9999px, creating a local value-label mismatch.
Many external globals are assumed to exist, including React, CanvasHeader, ViShape, RefinePop, window.claude, window.dsaToast, and navigator.clipboard.
notes: This file is the editable motion-and-space token workspace, with Vi-assisted proposal flows and local preview composition.
questions: Should generated token values be validated against the active token type before adoption?
Should r-pill display 999px, 9999px, or a named pill value consistently?
Should snapshot previews include all spacing and radii tokens or only the subset currently mapped in PatternPreviewBlock?
Should failed clipboard writes surface an error state?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it covers spacing, radii, shadow, and motion tokens with editing controls and previews.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make abstract pattern tokens tangible by pairing direct controls, lockable constraints, AI proposal helpers, and component previews that respond immediately to edits.
novelty: The distinct element is the combination of token editing, lock-aware AI generation, shadow-specific AI refinement, and snapshot-local CSS variable previews in one canvas.
contribution: Contributes a file-local pattern-token editor for spacing, radii, shadows, and motion, including base token definitions, mutation helpers, lock behavior, AI proposal adoption, shadow refinement, and live preview examples that a fused system could preserve as an interactive substrate ledger view.
summary_for_embedding: React canvas for ConceptV motion-and-space tokens with editable spacing, radii, shadows, motion, Vi generation/refinement, token locking, snapshots, copying, and live previews.
intention_for_embedding: Built to let users inspect and tune pattern design tokens while keeping the ConceptV paper-first aesthetic and locked-token constraints visible in the workflow.

symbols.does: Renders the main motion-and-space token canvas and coordinates tab state, generation, adoption, snapshots, and panel selection.
symbols.description: This component owns UI state for the active tab, Vi generation panel, prompt text, loading state, and generated proposal. It defines shared helpers for reading edits, checking locks, copying token references, generating proposals, and adopting proposed token values, then renders the header, generator, tab bar, active token panel, and live preview.

symbols.does: Returns an edited token value when present, otherwise returns the provided fallback.
symbols.description: This helper reads from the local edits object using nullish coalescing. It centralizes how token panels resolve the currently displayed value while preserving fallback defaults when no edit exists.

symbols.does: Checks whether a token name is included in the locked token list.
symbols.description: This helper normalizes a missing locked array to an empty array and tests membership by token name. Panels and adoption helpers use it to disable edits or skip generated values.

symbols.does: Copies a string to the clipboard and shows a toast naming the copied value.
symbols.description: This helper uses optional clipboard access and then calls the global toast function if present. It is used by token rows to copy CSS variable references such as var(--s-4).

symbols.does: Requests Vi-generated token proposals for the active tab and stores parsed entries as a proposal.
symbols.description: This async function builds a prompt with ConceptV brand constraints, active token focus, locked-token instructions, and the user's prompt. It calls window.claude.complete, attempts to parse compact JSON from the reply, filters entries with names and values, and reports parse or request failures through toasts.

symbols.does: Applies one generated token value unless that token is locked.
symbols.description: This helper checks the lock state before calling setEdit for a generated entry. It uses toasts to report locked-token rejection or successful adoption.

symbols.does: Applies all unlocked generated proposal entries and closes the proposal flow.
symbols.description: This helper iterates through proposal entries, skips locked tokens, writes each unlocked value with setEdit, clears the proposal, closes the generation panel, and reports how many tokens were adopted.

symbols.does: Renders editable spacing token rows and a stacked-card rhythm preview.
symbols.description: This component maps spacing tokens into rows with name, lock button, proportional bar, numeric input, copy action, and revert action for edited values. It also renders a property-card preview that uses spacing CSS variables for padding, margins, and button gaps.

symbols.does: Renders editable radius token swatches with lock, copy, and revert controls.
symbols.description: This component displays each radius token as a swatch with a gold shape preview whose border radius responds to the current value. It provides numeric editing for non-pill radii, shows a fixed pill label for r-pill, and marks edited tokens visually.

symbols.does: Renders shadow token cards with previews, text editing, copy, revert, lock, and Vi refinement.
symbols.description: This component maps shadow tokens into cards that show a small surface using the current box-shadow value. Each card exposes a textarea for direct CSS editing, lock-aware disabling, a copy action, optional revert, and a RefinePop workflow that asks Vi to return a revised shadow value as JSON.

symbols.does: Renders easing and duration token editors with animated motion previews.
symbols.description: This component splits motion tokens into easing and duration groups, displays cubic-bezier curves for easings, and animates dots to demonstrate timing. It provides direct editing, lock controls, copy and revert actions, replay controls, and an embedded keyframes rule for the sliding animation.

symbols.does: Parses a cubic-bezier CSS string into four numeric control values or null.
symbols.description: This helper extracts the comma-separated values inside cubic-bezier(...), parses them as floats, and returns the four-number array only when all parts are valid numbers. MotionPanel uses it to draw the SVG easing curve and choose an animation path.

symbols.does: Renders a combined preview block using local token CSS variables from an edit set.
symbols.description: This component builds a local style object from supplied edits and fallback token values so the preview can represent either live edits or a saved snapshot. It renders a card, tags, popover, and motion strip to show spacing, radii, shadows, and motion working together.

symbols.does: Returns a preview edit value when present, otherwise returns the supplied fallback.
symbols.description: This helper is local to PatternPreviewBlock and mirrors the main get helper for snapshot-safe preview values. It keeps the preview's CSS custom property map independent from global root token edits.

symbols.does: Renders a clickable animated strip using a supplied easing and duration.
symbols.description: This component keeps a replay key in state and increments it on click to restart a dot animation. It displays the duration label inside the strip and uses the passed easing and duration in the animation declaration.
